Title: The Quiet Ones
On-Screen Text: the bruises everyone asks about / are never the ones that broke you
Caption: nobody tells you the worst part isn't the yelling. it's the silence after. the apology that isn't one. the way you start editing yourself before you even open your mouth. you don't lose yourself all at once. you lose yourself in the rooms where you stopped speaking. if this is you, you are not crazy. you are not too sensitive. you are surviving something that doesn't always leave a mark. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Slow zoom on hands. Pink filter overlay. Serif headline.

Title: DARVO
On-Screen Text: he hit me / then called the police on me / for reacting
Caption: they spend months convincing you you're crazy in private. then perform sanity for an officer in five minutes flat. you stand on your own front porch explaining yourself for a script you didn't write. reactive abuse is real. DARVO is real. the woman shaking on the curb is not the aggressor. she's the witness he's trying to discredit. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Black background. White type. Pink accent line under each phrase.

Title: The Erosion
On-Screen Text: it wasn't one bad day / it was a thousand small surrenders
Caption: nobody warns you that abuse can be quiet. that it can wear a wedding ring. that it can say good morning and ruin your nervous system by noon. the version of you that used to laugh, gone. the friends you stopped calling. the way you started apologizing for taking up space in your own life. that's not a bad relationship. that's a slow erasure. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: 6-slide carousel. One line per slide. Pink-to-white gradient.

Title: What You Called Love
On-Screen Text: what you called love / my body called a warning
Caption: your shoulders never dropped around him. your stomach knew before your mind did. you were not paranoid. you were not insecure. you were a woman whose body was begging her to leave a man her heart kept defending. trust the tightness in your chest. it has always been smarter than the story you were telling yourself. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Voiceover. B-roll of soft hands

Title: Suffering In Silence
On-Screen Text: she was screaming / nobody heard / because she did it / with her mouth closed
Caption: the women suffering the loudest are often the quietest at brunch. she's funny. she's put together. she's the one who texts back. and she's been crying in her car for eight months and you have no idea. check on the friend who went quiet. believe her the first time. don't ask why she stayed, ask why he hurt her. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Pink square. Centered black serif. Small ♡ - MK bottom right.

Title: The Lie Of The Good Ones
On-Screen Text: he wasn't a monster / that was the problem / monsters are easier to leave
Caption: nobody hands you a leaflet that says abusers can be charming. they can be funny. they can cry. they can make your mother love them. they can hold your hand at a funeral and break your spirit by tuesday. the cognitive dissonance is the cage. the man who hurt you and the man you fell for live in the same body. leaving him means grieving both. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: 4-slide carousel. White background. Pink underline accents.

Title: The Rewrite
On-Screen Text: he wrote me as the villain / so i picked up the pen
Caption: for a long time i lived inside his version of me. crazy. dramatic. too much. not enough. every word he handed me, i wore. then one day i realized the story was still being written. and the pen was never his to hold. you don't have to defend yourself to the people who profited from your silence. you just have to start writing the next page. rebrand your life. rewrite your story. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Hand writing in a pink-tinted notebook. Slow tilt up.

Title: The Night You Left
On-Screen Text: the night i left / wasn't brave / it was the night i ran out of versions of myself / to give him
Caption: people will ask why you stayed. fewer will ask what it cost you to leave. you didn't leave because you got strong. you left because there was nothing left of you to bargain with. surviving is not a feeling. it's a decision you make in a kitchen at 2am with shaking hands. and it counts. all of it counts. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Black tile. Pink hairline border. Small heart at bottom.

Title: The Words He Gave You
On-Screen Text: every cruel name he called you / was a confession / dressed up as an accusation
Caption: crazy. dramatic. unstable. too emotional. too much. not enough. those weren't descriptions of you. those were rehearsals of him. you can put down every word he handed you. they were never yours to carry. the woman he tried to convince you you were is not the woman who survived him. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Words appear and dissolve one at a time on a soft pink ground.

Title: What Healing Looks Like
On-Screen Text: healing is not a glow-up / it's flinching less / when someone raises their voice
Caption: nobody tells you healing is small. it's the night you sleep through. the text you don't answer. the apology you stop waiting for. the meal you eat without explaining yourself. it's not pretty. it's not linear. it's not content. it's a woman, alone in her own kitchen, slowly remembering she is allowed to take up the whole room. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: White tile. Black serif. One pink line under healing.

Title: The Memory Test
On-Screen Text: first he rewrote the fight / then he rewrote you
Caption: gaslighting doesn't start with a lie. it starts with a small correction. that's not what i said. you're remembering it wrong. you're being dramatic. by the time you notice, you've stopped trusting your own memory. you've outsourced your reality to the person breaking you. take it back. one true sentence at a time. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Glitch text effect on the on-screen quote. Pink chromatic shift.

Title: Coercive Control 101
On-Screen Text: he never hit me / he just made sure i couldn't breathe
Caption: coercive control looks like permission for a haircut. like a budget you don't see. like a phone he checks in his sleep. like friends he pruned for you. like a god he made of himself in your living room. it is abuse. it is illegal in some places and should be in all of them. and you are not exaggerating. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: 6 slides. Each defines one tactic. Soft pink corners.

Title: The Friends You Lost
On-Screen Text: he didn't take your friends / he made staying friends with you cost too much
Caption: abusers don't burn the village. they raise the price of admission. by the time you look up, the people who loved you most are the ones you owe the most apologies to. for the calls you didn't return. the dinners you bailed on. the way you got quiet at the table. they will forgive you. start with one. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Slow pan over an empty dinner table. One chair pulled out.

Title: One True Sentence
On-Screen Text: start with one true sentence / he was not safe
Caption: when you can't write the whole story, write one true sentence. he was not safe. i was not crazy. i deserved better. that's a foundation. the rest of the book will come. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Pink card. Black serif. Slight paper texture.

Title: Trust Yourself First
On-Screen Text: the people who told you you were crazy / had a vested interest in your silence
Caption: consider the source. the people who needed you to doubt yourself were the ones profiting from your doubt. your gut was not the problem. your gut was the only honest thing in the room. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Mirror reflection. Soft pink light. Slow blink in.

Title: The First 30 Days
On-Screen Text: day one of no contact / is the loudest silence of your life
Caption: no contact is not closure. it's withdrawal. you will romanticize him on day three. you will draft a text on day seven. you will almost break on day fourteen. and on day thirty you will notice the lightness. it's not gone. but it's coming. hold the line. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Calendar carousel. Day 1 through Day 30. Pink check marks.

Title: Who You Were Before Him
On-Screen Text: there was a girl before him / and she's still in there / waiting to be picked up
Caption: before him you laughed louder. you wrote things. you wore the dress. you said the thing. she didn't die. she just got quiet. go back for her. she's been sitting on the curb of your old life this whole time. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Cut between old photos and present hands. Pink film grain.

Title: Believe Her The First Time
On-Screen Text: she will only tell you the polite version / believe her anyway
Caption: when a woman finally tells you, she has already shrunk it for your comfort. believe the polite version. then sit with her until she can tell you the real one. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Black tile. White serif. Pink heart pin.

Title: Love Bombing
On-Screen Text: the high was the bait / the low was the point
Caption: love bombing is not love. it's a deposit. they're collecting interest later. when the cold comes, you will chase the warmth and call it your fault. it was the plan. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Heat-map style overlay. Hot pink fading to gray.

Title: What Family Court Won't Tell You
On-Screen Text: he will look calm / and you will look hysterical / it is the design
Caption: family court is not a healing space. it is a performance. document everything. text in writing. screenshots backed up off your phone. find a lawyer who has handled coercive control. you will be told to be calm. be calm and be relentless. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: 5 slides of practical tips. Pink notebook aesthetic.

Title: The Body Keeps Score
On-Screen Text: your body remembered him / before your mind let you,
Caption: you will flinch at a slammed cabinet for years. you will brace at a familiar cologne. that's not weakness. that's a nervous system that learned to survive a person. it will recalibrate. give it time it didn't get the first time. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Soft focus on hands unclenching. Slow exhale audio.

Title: Your Anger Is Information
On-Screen Text: your anger is not the problem / it's the proof
Caption: you weren't an angry woman. you were a woman who finally figured out what was being done to her. anger is not a flaw. it's a compass. follow it home. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Pink tile. One bold black headline. Tiny ♡ - MK.

Title: The Strong Friend
On-Screen Text: the strong friend is not okay / she's just well-rehearsed
Caption: the woman everyone leans on is the woman nobody checks on. she will tell you she's fine because she's been performing fine since she was seven. ask twice. mean it. let the silence get long. she'll fill it eventually. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Quiet kitchen scene. Tea steam rising. Pink-tinted window.

Title: The Apology That Wasn't
On-Screen Text: i'm sorry you feel that way / is not an apology / it's a closing argument
Caption: real apologies have three parts. i did this. it hurt you in this way. here is what i will do differently. anything less is a lawyer with feelings. you do not have to accept the closing argument. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: 4 slides. Each defines one element of a real apology.

Title: Financial Abuse Is Abuse
On-Screen Text: he didn't lock the door / he locked the bank account
Caption: financial abuse is rarely on the brochure. allowance. veto power. credit run into the ground in your name. a job he made impossible to keep. it counts. it's a cage. start a separate account today. even ten dollars. that's the first key. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Tight shot of a hand opening a banking app. Pink UI overlay.

Title: You Were Never Too Much
On-Screen Text: you were never too much / you were just with someone too small
Caption: loud. emotional. ambitious. needy. those were not flaws. those were the symptoms of being underloved by someone who needed you smaller. take up the room. the right people will pull up a chair. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Pink card. Centered serif. Subtle paper grain.

Title: When Family Picks Him
On-Screen Text: the second betrayal / is the one with your last name
Caption: sometimes the people who should have caught you, didn't. sometimes they sided with him because his story was easier than yours. that grief is real. you can love them and still build a life that doesn't need their permission. chosen family is family. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Empty frame on a wall. Pink ribbon tied to it.

Title: A Quiet Safety Plan
On-Screen Text: you don't have to leave today / you have to be ready for the day you can
Caption: copy of your ID somewhere safe. cash you can reach. a friend who knows the code word. screenshots in a hidden album. a bag at your sister's. a number for the local hotline. you are not paranoid. you are preparing. that's wisdom. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: 6 slides of practical checklist items. Pink check marks.

Title: Talk To Yourself Like Her
On-Screen Text: the way you talk to yourself / is the last room he's allowed to live in
Caption: kick him out of that one too. the inner critic with his voice. the eye roll in your head. the predictions of your failure that sound exactly like him. he doesn't get the inside of your skull. evict him. one kind sentence at a time. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Mirror shot. Pink lipstick writing on the glass.

Title: A True Sentence
On-Screen Text: i was not crazy / i was being mistreated / both can be true
Caption: sometimes both things are true. you may have your own things to work on. and you were still being mistreated. one does not cancel the other. don't let your work on yourself become evidence in his case against you. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Pink tile. Black serif. Tiny heart sign.

Title: The Person He Built
On-Screen Text: i spent ten years being the woman / he could tolerate / and she still wasn't enough
Caption: you cannot shrink yourself into being loved. you can only shrink yourself into being kept. there is a difference. one of them is a life. the other is a holding cell with good lighting. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Slow zoom out on a small chair in a big room.

Title: The Apology Tour
On-Screen Text: start with one friend / say one true sentence / let her say the rest
Caption: when you come back to your people, you don't have to explain everything. you can say i wasn't well and i missed you. most of them have been waiting. the ones who haven't, were never the village. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: 4 slides of suggested first texts. Pink message bubbles.

Title: Find The Right Therapist
On-Screen Text: the wrong therapist / will hand you back to him / in better language
Caption: not every therapist understands coercive control. some will couples-counsel you back into a cage. find one trained in trauma, narcissistic abuse, or DV. it is the difference between getting better and getting gaslit with credentials. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Notepad with checklist of what to ask in a consult.

Title: The Standard
On-Screen Text: the bar is not / does he hit me / the bar is / does he make me feel safe
Caption: the bar is not the absence of cruelty. it's the presence of safety. raise it. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Bold pink horizontal line on a white tile. Black serif overhead.

Title: Closure Is A Myth
On-Screen Text: closure is what you give yourself / when the apology never comes
Caption: you will not get the conversation. you will not get the apology. you will not get the version where he understands what he did. closure is not a door he walks through. it's one you close. you have the key. you've had it the whole time. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Hand turning a key. Pink rim light.

Title: If You Have Kids With Him
On-Screen Text: the kids are watching / not the fights / they are watching how you recover
Caption: you cannot make him a better father. you can only model what a steady adult looks like. the rage, the explanation, the panic, those go to your therapist and your friends. they do not go in front of the kids. that's the rewrite. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: 4 slides of grounded coparenting principles.

Title: The Loneliness After
On-Screen Text: the loneliness after / is louder than the relationship / and it is still safer
Caption: you will miss him at 2am. you will miss the noise. you will miss the version of him that didn't exist. that's not a sign to go back. that's withdrawal. it ends. the quiet becomes peace. give it time. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Window at night. Soft pink streetlight. Slow rain.

Title: Worthy In The Wreckage
On-Screen Text: worthy in the wreckage / worthy in the rebuild / worthy on a tuesday with no makeup on
Caption: your worth was never up for review. not in his court. not in your family's court. not in your own. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: White tile. Pink underline. Bold black serif.

Title: The People Who Believed Him
On-Screen Text: the people who believed him / told you everything / about themselves
Caption: you don't have to win them back. their belief in his story is a confession of who they are. let them go. you are building a life. they are still in his audience. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Pink curtain falling. Stage emptying.

Title: The Pattern
On-Screen Text: if it happened once / it was an event / if it happened three times / it was a pattern
Caption: abusers test. they apologize. they try again. they escalate. they apologize bigger. you start tracking weather instead of behavior. write it down. dates, words, the temperature in the room. patterns are evidence. memory alone won't hold. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: 4 slides. Notebook aesthetic. Pink tabs.

Title: The Return Of Joy
On-Screen Text: joy is the most rebellious thing / a survivor can do
Caption: they wanted you small. joy is the loudest no you can give. eat the cake. dance in the kitchen. wear the dress. laugh too loud at brunch. that is the rewrite, in real time. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Sunlight through a window. Hands in flour. Pink tones.

Title: The Light
On-Screen Text: there is a version of you / on the other side of this / and she is so glad you stayed
Caption: she's not perfect. she's not healed all the way. but she's here. she's funny again. she sleeps through the night. she dates a man who texts back. she is real. she is waiting. don't leave her behind. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Pink sunrise tile. Centered serif. Tiny heart.

Title: Rebrand Your Life
On-Screen Text: rebrand your life / rewrite your story / they don't get the next chapter
Caption: this is the work. this is the dispatch. this is what we do here. you take back the pen. you change the tone. you fire the people in your story who never deserved a speaking part. you rebrand. it is allowed. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Cut sequence. Old photos. New photos. Pink film leader between.

Title: Five Things To Do This Week
On-Screen Text: small moves / steady hands / no drama
Caption: one. open a separate bank account. two. screenshot anything you might need. three. tell one safe person. four. eat a real meal. five. write one true sentence. that's a week. that's how it starts. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: 5 slides of one action each. Pink check marks.

Title: Your Voice Was The Threat
On-Screen Text: he wasn't afraid of your tears / he was afraid of your voice
Caption: your voice was always the threat. that's why he worked so hard to make you doubt it. use it now. quietly. on the page. in a journal. in a text to a friend. on a stage. the volume doesn't matter. the using does. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Microphone in soft pink light. Slow pull focus.

Title: A Note For The Sister Reading This
On-Screen Text: if you're reading this in a parking lot / hiding from the man inside the house / you are not alone / and you are not crazy
Caption: you are tired. you are scared. you are still here. that is not nothing. that is the whole thing. the hotline is 1-800-799-7233. text START to 88788. there are people whose only job is to help. let one of them. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Pink card. Hotline numbers in black bold serif. Heart sign.

Title: The Woman In The Mirror
On-Screen Text: the woman in the mirror / has been waiting for you / to come home to her
Caption: he kept you out of your own house for years. the door is unlocked. she left a light on. go in. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Mirror. Soft pink lamp. Slow walk-in shot.

Title: Subscribe
On-Screen Text: if this hit / there is more / quietly / weekly / signed M.K.
Caption: the dispatch is where i write the long version. raw and poetic. no algorithm. no performance. just sentences for the women rewriting their lives in real time. link in bio. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: 3 slides. Pink envelope motif. Subtle scroll cue.

Title: The Quiet Ones (Ebook)
On-Screen Text: the quiet ones / a rewrite for women who survived what no one saw / out now
Caption: ten chapters. ten poems. ten rewrites. the book i wish someone had handed me at 2am in the kitchen. it's not pretty. it's honest. it's yours if you want it. link in bio. ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Hand placing the ebook cover on a pink linen table.

Title: The Last Line
On-Screen Text: they wrote me as the villain / so i picked up the pen / and the rest of this story / is mine
Caption: ♡ - MK
Visual Note: Black tile. Pink hairline border. Bold white serif. Heart sign center.
